title: Java Reference Null
description: Practice null checks before dereferencing reference values.
learningGoals: Recognize the dereference that would throw NullPointerException.

completionChecks.prompt: What does this code do when findName(id) returns null?
choices: It prints 0 for the length of a null name
It silently skips the print and continues
It throws NullPointerException with the message "missing name"
It throws NullPointerException at name.length() instead
completionChecks.explanation: When name is null the if branch runs and explicitly throws NullPointerException("missing name"), so execution never reaches name.length().

completionChecks.prompt: Which operation can throw NullPointerException here?
choices: Dereferencing with name.length() when name is null
The throw statement, because new always throws
completionChecks.explanation: Comparing a reference to null is always safe; dereferencing a null reference by calling a method on it is what throws NullPointerException.

choices: A NullPointerException with message "missing name" is thrown
Compilation fails because name might still be null
completionChecks.explanation: The null check is skipped for a non-null String, and name.length() returns 3 for Ada.
